text:
Deep learning has achieved incredible successes in a wide range of applications, from computer vision to speech recognition to natural language processing. As our models get more accurate, they are also becoming more complex. They are often characterized by a large number of parameters, slow inference times, and high computational and memory requirements. It is simply not acceptable anymore to focus solely on the accuracy of a model. The conversation has to change to how we can develop models that are not only accurate but also efficient and lean.

This survey is a tour through the efficient deep learning landscape. We start by discussing what it means to be efficient and then dive into the five pillars of efficiency which are: - Compression - Learning - Automation - Models - Infrastructure (Software and Hardware) We provide an experimental guide and corresponding code that can serve as a practical resource for any practitioner looking to build efficient models that can be used in training and in production. We combine the art of modeling with the science of current hardware to provide a mental model that yields instant gains and a future research direction that aims to lead to the optimal performance possible.
